title: Ordered-boosting raw-category specialist with cross features
summary: Current best snippets still rely mostly on manual frequency encodings.
rationale: Current best snippets still rely mostly on manual frequency encodings. CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics were designed specifically to reduce prediction shift from target leakage in high-cardinality categoricals, which fits Driver, Race, Year, Compound, and their interactions.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost specialist on mostly raw categoricals plus a few numeric anchors, including crosses like Driver x Race, Driver x Compound, Race x Compound, and Race x Year. Keep native categorical handling, avoid manual one-hot, and blend by rank with the existing AutoGluon/tabular base instead of replacing it outright.
expected_effect: Should recover interaction signal from sparse driver/race identities that manual counts and medians miss, while being more robust than fold-global encodings.
risk: If folds are not grouped by race-driver chronology, even ordered boosting can look better locally than it generalizes.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
